top_signals.title: Shipping stalls in Strait of Hormuz after Iran declares key waterway closed again
top_signals.summary: The update comes even as industry trackers showed Iranian tankers have continued to sail through the strait.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Sprake: SARB Likely to Hike Before Year-End
top_signals.summary: Traders are paring back their bets on further interest-rate hikes by the South African Reserve Bank after inflation came in lower than expected. But Casey Sprake, Market Strategist at AG Capital told Bloomberg’s Chief Africa Correspondent Jennifer Zabasajja, they are expecting another rate hike before the end of the year. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: The Price of Everything is Skyrocketing — Including Ice Cream
top_signals.summary:
This week, the Everybody's Business podcast hits the streets to ask New Yorkers how much they're paying for an ice cream cone.

Bloomberg's David Papadopoulos joins Stacey Vanek Smith and Max Chafkin to discuss how more and more things are out of reach for many Americans as inflation continues to outpace wages. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: SpaceX stock falls 9%, pacing for third-straight day of losses after red-hot IPO start
top_signals.summary: A rally following the company's record breaking IPO on June 12 has cooled, with stock dropping the past two full days of trading.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: SpaceX signs computing power deal with open-source AI startup Reflection worth up to $6.3 billion
top_signals.summary: SpaceX has turned its Colossus data center into a commercial computing power platform, landing recent deals with Anthropic, Google and Cursor.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

alerts.title: U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, April 2026
alerts.summary: The U.S. monthly international trade deficit decreased in April 2026 according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau. The deficit decreased from $56.6 billion in March (revised) to $55.9 billion in April, as exports increased more than imports. The goods deficit decreased $2.4 billion in April to $83.7 billion. The services surplus decreased $1.7 billion in April to $27.8 billion. Full Text
